feat: add retry logic with exponential backoff to API calls

Resolves #7

_get_json now retries up to 3 times on URLError or non-200 responses,
with exponential backoff (1s, 2s) between attempts.

commit.message:
feat(#8): travel time integration & leave-now alerts

- TravelTimeService: Google Directions API with real-time traffic,
  5-min cache, fallback estimation when API unavailable
- calculateTravelBlocks: auto-insert travel blocks between events
- Leave-now departure tracking with minutes-until-departure
- Maps deep-link (tap to open Google Maps for directions)
- TravelBlockCard component: visual route display with urgency states,
  traffic delay badges, and directions button
- TravelSettings component: default travel mode selector (drive/transit/walk/bike)
- useTravelTime hook: auto-refresh every 5min, upcoming departures filter
- Configurable via EXPO_PUBLIC_GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY env var

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(#6): multi-calendar import & sync with Google, Outlook, Apple

- Calendar provider interface with Google OAuth, Outlook OAuth, Apple EventKit
- CalendarSyncService: connect/disconnect, toggle calendars, auto-sync every 15min
- ICS file import parser (VEVENT → ExternalEvent)
- useCalendarSync hook for React components
- Calendar Sync settings screen with connect/disconnect, calendar toggles, sync status
- expo-auth-session + expo-crypto + expo-calendar dependencies

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add G21 Faction System: types, territories, relations, and manager

Implement the Faction System (G21) with:
- FactionType enum (8 faction types) and FactionStanding enum (5 levels)
- FactionTerritory struct with erosion/reinforcement mechanics
- Faction class with territory, wealth, influence, and tick management
- GameFactionRelation struct with reputation-driven standing transitions,
  war/peace, and treaty support
- GameFactionManager class with faction CRUD, relation tracking, and
  allied/hostile faction queries
- 10 comprehensive Catch2 test cases covering all functionality

Note: Relation and Manager types prefixed with 'Game' to avoid naming
conflicts with existing AI module faction types.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: shifty81 <50773914+shifty81@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(M1): add GLFW/ImGui window support for the editor

Add GLFWWindowProvider, ImGuiLayer, ImGuiBackend, GLFWInputAdapter
stub implementations and update main.cpp with NF_USE_GLFW path.
Includes 23 new Catch2 tests covering all M1 components.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: shifty81 <50773914+shifty81@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(#5): add backend API architecture and infrastructure

Backend (Express + TypeScript + PostgreSQL):
- RESTful routes: /api/auth, /api/users, /api/events, /api/family
- JWT authentication middleware with token generation
- PostgreSQL schema with migrations (users, events, families, travel_blocks)
- Request validation with Zod schemas
- Security: helmet, CORS, rate limiting
- Environment config (dev/staging/prod)

Frontend:
- API client service with typed methods for all endpoints
- Excluded backend/ from root tsconfig

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(#19): add security, privacy compliance, and biometric auth

- SecureStore service: iOS Keychain token storage with web fallback
- Biometric auth service: Face ID / Touch ID via expo-local-authentication
- Input validation/sanitization: email, password strength, XSS prevention
- Privacy Policy screen with GDPR/CCPA rights section
- Terms of Service screen
- Registered new routes in root layout

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(#21): add testing strategy with Jest and React Native Testing Library

- Jest config with jest-expo preset, path aliases, 70% coverage threshold
- Unit tests: conflict detection, formatDuration, getGreeting (dataTransforms)
- Unit tests: mockData validation (structure, referential integrity)
- Added test/test:watch/test:coverage npm scripts
- 3 suites, 17 tests all passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 10 — shifty81/AtlasForge-EveOffline (56dd9f4):

  - 22 addon Python files → Archive/_Blender-Generator-for-AtlasForge/addon_source/
  - 9 docs → Archive/_Blender-Generator-for-AtlasForge/docs/
  - Archived separately from Tools/BlenderGenerator/ (Phase 4 content)

Both ARCHIVE_SUMMARY.md files updated with full audit.

feat(#27): add global error handling and graceful degradation

- GlobalErrorBoundary: catches React errors with friendly UI and retry
- NetworkBanner: auto-detects offline/online with animated banner
- ToastProvider: context-based toast notifications (success/error/warning/info)
- retryWithBackoff: exponential backoff utility for API calls
- logger: level-aware logging (verbose in dev, errors-only in prod)

- Wired DataProvider into root layout

- TestFlight submission on production builds

Add comprehensive AtlasForge audit report for consolidation

Audit of shifty81/AtlasForge repository (SHA: 2cafe95) covering:
- Full directory structure with file counts for all 14 top-level dirs
- engine/ explored 2 levels deep (34 subdirs, ~290 files)
- Content category analysis for all directories
- Overlap analysis against Phase 3 and Phase 4 extractions
- Extraction recommendations: 18 EXTRACT, 685 ARCHIVE, 15 SKIP

Key finding: AtlasForge is the primary origin repo already consumed
by Phases 3-4. Remaining value is ~18 potentially new files plus
the full archive as reference material.

SLAM3D modular refactoring - Phase 1

- Create SLAM3D/ folder structure with modular components
- Add PoseState.cs with anti-jitter filtering (fixes rubberbanding)
- Add VoxelStorage.cs with occlusion tracking (dynamic object removal)
- Add renderers: Grid, Voxel, Trajectory, Detection, Drone
- Add WebSocketClient with auto-reconnect
- Add CameraController (orbit/FPS/third-person modes)
- Add CoordinateConverter for ROS<->OpenGL frame conversion
- Add MathHelper for .NET Framework 4.8 compatibility
- Add SLAM3DManager integration layer
- Add magnetometer stats to ros_http_bridge get_stats()
- Enable sensors.publish_mag in ZED config (requires node restart)

Key improvements:
- Position jumps >0.35m are rejected (prevents map shifting)
- Yaw jumps >25 degrees are rejected
- Voxels are removed after 3 consecutive unseen frames
- Magnetometer yaw ready (pending ZED restart)

Build: Compiles successfully
Test: Jetson accessible, config updated

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve 17 pipeline audit findings (F-01 through F-17)

Blocking:
- F-04: gas-city.md session close includes wasteland push
- F-07: slides corrected from 'two gates' to 'three gates'
- F-08: slide agent table aligned to formula agent names
- F-09: rules-engine rig uncommented in city.toml

Should-fix:
- F-01: code-reviewer stamp mechanism for external completions
- F-02: backlog-planner auto-INSERTs missing wanted items
- F-03: doc-sync auto-posts new ADRs to wasteland decisions
- F-05: backlog-planner session close includes wasteland push
- F-06: session close sections added to 6 missing prompts
- F-10: Catala step conditionality documented in troubleshooting

Cosmetic:
- F-11: start-beads.sh (Linux equivalent of start-beads.ps1)
- F-12: bd prime reference in developer-guide.md
- F-13: hardcoded paths replaced with git rev-parse + jq
- F-14: hardcoded HOP_DIR replaced with jq config lookup
- F-15: 3 new wasteland patterns (gdpr-pii-silo, api-first, catala)
- F-16: single rig accepted (expected for single-dev setup)
- F-17: <topic> placeholder documented in petition-to-gherkin

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore(packs): align agent prompts to full 10-step 3-gate pipeline

- Add pipeline position header comment to all 7 agent prompts so each
  agent knows where it sits in the molecule workflow
- tdd-enforcer: remove incorrect bd update --assignee before closing;
  in molecule context the formula handles routing automatically
- doc-sync: add wasteland sync step (completions INSERT + wanted UPDATE
  to done + dolt push) after program-status.yaml is set to implemented
- backlog-planner: add wasteland claim step when activating a petition
  bead (UPDATE wanted SET status=in_progress, dolt push)
- code-reviewer: explicitly call out ArchUnit tests in review checklist
  (ENTITIES_MUST_NOT_STORE_PII + noAccessToOtherServiceRepositories)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: expand Gas City pipeline to full 10-step 3-gate flow

Replace the abbreviated pipeline summary with the complete step-by-step
flow derived from mol-petition-scaffold and mol-petition-implement formulas:

Phase 1: translate → gherkin + specs (parallel) → ⏸ Gate 1 (scaffold review)
Phase 2: implement → review → catala-encode (Tier A) → ⏸ Gate 2 (code review)
         → doc-sync → ⏸ Gate 3 (merge gate)

Each gate now has a dedicated section with a review table, approve and
reject commands, and post-merge cleanup steps. Three gates documented
(not two — the merge gate is a full human gate, not a footnote).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: clarify automatic vs manual petition submission in Gas City guide

The 'Submitting a petition' section now explicitly documents the normal
path (set status: ready_for_implementation in program-status.yaml and
let backlog-planner pick it up automatically) vs the manual bd create
path (one-off petitions not tracked in program-status.yaml).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs(developer-guide): add Wasteland, Gas City, and Beads to contribution workflow

- Contribution workflow section expanded: Beads/Wasteland/program-status
  granularity table, bd ready, wasteland wanted board query
- Gas City subsection: gc start/stop/bd gates quick-ref, link to gas-city.md
- Manual contribution subsection preserved with conventional commit format
- 'Marking work complete in Wasteland' subsection with dolt commands
- Session close checklist: git + bd + wasteland dolt push
- index.md: add Gas City to Contents list

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs(slides): own the inversion — reframe human role as governance, not participation

- Spectrum table: add 'Human role | Writes the code | Governs the process'
- New slide 'The Inversion': explicitly names the role reversal, shows what
  each human gate demands (professional judgment, legal accountability, scope
  authority); closes with 'Two gates. All the accountability. None of the
  grunt work.'
- Core Insight table: reframe human gate row from passive to active
  ('judgment concentrated at accountability moments, not diluted across every PR')
- Takeaway: reframe tagline to 'knowing what only a human should decide';
  pipeline now shows ⏸ Human Gate as explicit step

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs(slides): add Gas City and Wasteland to ai-driven-development.md

- Pipeline diagram: add Wasteland as final step
- Step 4 retitled 'Gas City — Agent Orchestration' with gc/bd quick-ref
  and two mandatory human gates noted
- New slide: Step 6 'The Wasteland — Federated Knowledge'
  (rig, wanted, completion, pattern, stamp concepts)
- Core Insight table: +Gas City orchestration row, +Wasteland row
- Final tagline updated to include Gas City and Wasteland
- Thank-you footer stats updated

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add Gas City orchestration config for petition-driven pipeline

- city.toml: 7 city-scoped agents + daemon config + rig template
- packs/opendebt/pack.toml: pack manifest with schema=1, formulas dir
- packs/opendebt/formulas/mol-petition-scaffold.formula.toml:
    Phase 1: translate → gherkin+specs (parallel) → human gate
- packs/opendebt/formulas/mol-petition-implement.formula.toml:
    Phase 2: TDD → review → [catala] → human gate → doc-sync → merge gate
- packs/opendebt/formulas/orders/: gate-based dispatch orders
- packs/opendebt/prompts/: 8 domain-aware prompt templates derived from agents.md
- prompts/pool-worker.md: default pool worker prompt (gc requirement)
- orders/beads-health/: system health order (materialised by gc doctor --fix)

Mandatory human gates at scaffold review and merge prevent autonomous
agents from committing to main without explicit approval in this
regulated-domain (Danish public debt collection, €600M+ revenue).

To activate the pilot:
  gc rig add rules-engine /path/to/opendebt-rules-engine
  gc start ~/GitHub/opendebt

Co-authored-by: xadupre <22452781+xadupre@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix reloading issues (#24)

* fix: leads full-page reload and delete crash

Add <svelte:boundary> to the app layout to handle async pending/error
states for remote function queries. Without this boundary, pages using
$derived(await query()) would fail on full-page reload because there
was nothing to catch the pending state during hydration.

Fix lead deletion crash by using submit().updates() (single-flight
mutation with no queries) instead of bare submit(). The default
auto-invalidation was re-running getLeadData() for the just-deleted
lead, causing a 404 error before goto() could navigate away.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add regression tests for leads boundary and delete fix

Add 12 new tests (263 total, 0 failures):

- Layout boundary structural tests (5): verify svelte:boundary exists
  with pending and failed snippets, and that children render inside it.
  Prevents accidental removal of the boundary.

- Lead detail page tests (7): verify page renders correctly, and
  critically test that deleteLead.enhance() calls submit().updates()
  (not bare submit()) to prevent auto-invalidation crash. Also tests
  navigation to /leads after delete and error handling on failure.

Enhance createFormMock to capture enhance callbacks via _enhanceCallback
property, and add issues() support to field proxy.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(tests): fix lint errors and improve test patterns in regression tests

Replace _enhanceCallback hack with DOM-based interactions and remove all
as-any casts to satisfy @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any. Make
addFormApi generic to preserve Mock type through Object.assign. Improve
layout structural guard tests with regex matchers and better comments.

* chore: bump version to 0.2.4 in package.json and bun.lock

* fix: update button component to support loading state and enhance delete lead functionality

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs(agents): add wasteland integration instructions

- New ## Wasteland Integration section: Claim, Complete, Stamp, New items
- Session Completion: add 'dolt push origin main' for wasteland sync
- AIDEV workflow: backlog-planner also posts new TB items to wasteland wanted
- C4 Governance: new ADRs also go into wasteland decisions table

Local clone: ~/.hop/commons/mfhens/ufst | Remote: mfhens/ufst

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(wasteland): extend skill with patterns and learnings commands

Adds four new commands to the wasteland skill:

  patterns [filter]       — browse reusable patterns
  add-pattern [id]        — register a new pattern (slug-keyed)
  learnings [pattern-id]  — browse findings by pattern
  learn <pattern-id>      — record a new learning

Schema (already applied to mfhens/ufst on DoltHub):
  patterns  — slug-keyed, stamped for quality, categorized
  learnings — anchored to pattern + project context, with
              legal_basis field for petition/ADR traceability

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore(creditor-portal): ignore font files served from shared component

Font files under src/main/resources/static/fonts/ are now provided
by the skat-design-system shared component and should not be tracked
in the creditor-portal module.

- PanelHost: attach/detach, find, duplicate prevention
- DrawList: empty, push, clear

Co-authored-by: shifty81 <50773914+shifty81@users.noreply.github.com>

Add AtlasUI directory structure with theme, widgets, commands, and services

Create the complete AtlasUI subsystem under Source/UI/ adapted from
the Additions/ starter packs into the NF::UI::AtlasUI namespace:

- Types.h: Shared AtlasUI types (Rect, Vec2, WidgetState, SizeClass)
- Theme system: ThemeTypes, ThemeTokens, AtlasTheme, ThemeDefaults
  (Atlas Dark theme), ThemeManager singleton
- Widget infrastructure: Interfaces (IWidget, IPanel, IPopupHost),
  WidgetBase, PanelBase, PanelHost, Contexts, DrawPrimitives, helpers
- Widgets: Button, Panel, TabBar, Splitter, Toolbar, Tooltip,
  TextInput, Dropdown, PropertyRow, NotificationCard
- Command system: CommandTypes, CommandRegistry, CommandManager,
  ShortcutRouter, CommandHelpers, CommandDefaults
- Services: FocusService, PopupHost, TooltipService, NotificationHost
- Updated CMakeLists.txt with all new source files
- Moved spec rollup docs to Docs/SpecRollup/

All 672 existing tests continue to pass.

Add advanced box score, hustle, clutch, and matchup adapter functions

Add four new data adapter functions to bridge advanced NBA stats into
the engine projection pipeline:

- build_engine_advanced_context: aggregates advanced box score stats
  (USG_PCT, TS_PCT, PIE, PACE, etc.) and merges estimated metrics
- build_engine_hustle_context: averages hustle stats (deflections,
  contested shots, screen assists, box outs)
- build_engine_clutch_context: transforms clutch stats for close-game
  performance analysis
- build_engine_matchup_defender_context: aggregates individual defender
  matchup data to identify primary defenders

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Cursor/development environment setup 3671 (#133)

* Add Cursor Cloud specific instructions for Linux development

- Document Linux-specific environment setup (GPU acceleration, node-pty rebuild, spawn-helper compilation)
- Document dev mode race condition workaround
- Note cr-sqlite and computer-use feature limitations on Linux
- Add tips for running Electron app and tests efficiently

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix cr-sqlite sync API to gracefully degrade on Linux

The sync API methods (getDbVersion, exportChangesSince, applyChanges)
threw when cr-sqlite extension was unavailable, preventing project
initialization on platforms without the extension (Linux).

Changed to return safe no-op defaults (0, [], empty result) instead
of throwing, allowing the sync service to initialize harmlessly
without CRDT sync capability.

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove accidentally committed .ade db backup files

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix onboarding to trigger on new machines with tracked .ade directory

Check for ade.db (gitignored, machine-local) instead of the .ade
directory (tracked in git with shared config). This ensures onboarding
runs when a project is cloned to a new machine that has the shared
scaffold but no local runtime state.

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add env var fallback for GitHub, Linear, and AI API key credentials

Allows headless/CI/cloud environments to provide credentials via
environment variables when Electron's safeStorage encrypted store
is empty or unavailable.

- GitHub: GITHUB_TOKEN or ADE_GITHUB_TOKEN
- Linear: LINEAR_API_KEY or ADE_LINEAR_TOKEN
- AI providers: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY,
  MISTRAL_API_KEY, DEEPSEEK_API_KEY, XAI_API_KEY, GROQ_API_KEY,
  TOGETHER_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_API_KEY

The encrypted store remains the primary source. Env vars are only
checked as a fallback when no stored credential is found.

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Auto-complete onboarding when env var credentials are detected

When a project is opened for the first time on a new machine and
environment variables provide at least one credential (GitHub token,
AI API key, or Linear token), mark onboarding as complete so the
wizard is skipped. This lets cloud agents and CI environments that
set credentials via env vars go straight to the Work view.

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: expand AI provider env var check in onboarding auto-complete to cover all 9 supported providers

commit.message:
Add Shadow Lift preset using power-curve gamma algorithm

Implements the shadow lift algorithm with gamma=1.5 (exponent≈0.667).
The power curve raises dark tones more than bright tones while keeping
pure white anchored. Max deviation from identity is ~9,800 at i≈75,
well within the 32,000 driver safety limit.
